evals.prompt: Analyze my provider's acceptance tests and produce a parallel execution script. The test output from the last sequential run is at .claude/skills/provider-test-speed-workspace/test-output.log. Generate the script but do NOT execute it. Compare grouping strategy and estimated speedup vs the sequential baseline.
evals.expected_output: A runnable parallel execution script with 4-5 groups, pre-compiled binary, exponential backoff retry, and a comparison table showing estimated speedup.

assertions.description: Creates an executable bash script for parallel test execution
Script pre-compiles test binary with 'go test -c' before launching groups
Uses 4 or 5 parallel groups (not 3 or fewer, not 6+)
Tests triggering long-running backend operations are separated from CRUD-only and data source tests
Includes retry with exponential backoff for timeout-related failures
Test run patterns cover all discovered acceptance tests without gaps
Outputs a comparison table or estimate of sequential vs parallel wall times
